accept: root cause identified for aac-website case (config vs bundling bug)
if config: skill/onboarding updated to recommend per_session as default for repos with code
if bug: bundling logic fixed; regression test exercises the failing case
payload.description: aac-website dogfood ran 15+ close-then-git-commit two-step sequences. Per act-a659 and act-728d, bundle_strategy=per_session should fold the close op into the next code commit, eliminating the two-step. Three possible root causes: (a) aac-website is not on per_session (config or onboarding default issue); (b) bundling has a gap when close is called from a different working tree than the work; (c) staging did not fire when it should have. Investigate and either document (and update onboarding/skill to recommend per_session) or fix. Source: docs/aac-website-dogfood-debrief.md §1, §7.
payload.title: investigate close+commit fold gap — per_session did not collapse the two-step on aac-website dogfood
